notes.note: A very short label for the navbar map label
The aria label of the mark this spot as visited icon button
Mark this spot as visited aria label

segment.source: Icon button with a banner and checkmark icon

notes.note: The tooltip of the mark this spot as visited icon button
Mark this spot as visited tooltip
A very short label for the navbar about page button

segment.source: Add a name or some spot details before saving this new spot.
Create organizations and manage who can review organization-routed spot edits. Verified-by and managed-by settings live on each spot's admin edit form.
Choose the language used in the app.
Sign in to vote on this edit.
Training happens outside, not behind a desk. PK Spot works on any device — download the native app or install it from your browser.
Whether you are a solo athlete, a gym owner, or a national organization — we are happy to have you on board.
Multiple organizations can verify a public spot without owning it.
Review community reports and contact messages.
Crafted in Zürich, Switzerland. Open Data. Open Code. ©2026 PK Spot.

notes.note: Button to go to the account page
Button to go back to account

segment.source: Password must be at least 6 characters
This link is invalid or has expired. Please request a new one.
Latest open spot and media reports.
Unable to process this request. Please try again later.
Error, there was a problem signing out!
This link is not recognized. Please check your email and try again.
Please wait while we verify your email address...
Your email address has been successfully verified. You can now access all features of PK Spot.
Your password has been successfully reset. You can now sign in with your new password.
Please wait while we recover your email address...
Your email address has been successfully recovered. You may want to change your password if you didn't make this change.
This link has expired. Please request a new one.
This link is invalid or has already been used. Please request a new one.
This account has been disabled. Please contact support.
No account found for this email address.
The password is too weak. Please use at least 6 characters.
An error occurred. Please try again or request a new link.
This challenge is still private to you!
This challenge will be released on , at .
— We never sell your data. No tracking cookies, no shady ad networks. Privacy Policy.
1 edit is waiting for community voting.
— All community spot data is CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 licensed. Open, shareable, yours forever.
The Challenge will only be available starting from the release date. Before that it will be private to you.
The Challenge will be public right away after it is added.
Covered rails for rainy nights. Lit spots for winter sessions. Parkour gyms for drill days. Filter for exactly what you need.
We don't have a PK Spot community page for this route yet.
Link to challenge copied to clipboard

notes.note: The aria label of the bookmark spot icon button

segment.source: Icon button with a bookmark icon

notes.note: The tooltip of the bookmark spot icon button

segment.source: Drag the pin to the event location. Click the map to add an optional event area.
Drag the pin for the event location. Drag the area or move its points to fit the optional event area.
Tell us about jams, chats, classes, shops, or anything locals should know so we can add it here.
Help with testing, software, spot reviews, translations, design, or local community work.
Google My Maps, KML/KMZ files, spreadsheets, and local crew lists are all welcome starting points.
Please add contact information, up to 240 characters.
Email, Instagram, Discord, or whatever works best for you.
Prefer a community chat? Join the Discord and say hello there.
Quick question or casual hello? You can also reach us on Instagram.
Please add a message, up to 4000 characters.
We only use this information to reply to your message.
Contact PK Spot about support, spot map imports, partnerships, testing, or joining the crew.
Hi PK Spot, I have local knowledge for :
Hi PK Spot, I would like to help with:
Hi PK Spot, I have a spot map or list I would like to share:
Could not send your message. Please try again or reach out on Instagram.
Thanks for reaching out. We will get back to you through the contact info you shared.
Want to help with testing, spot reviews, translations, design, community work, or software? We'd love to hear where you want to pitch in.
Questions, ideas, bug reports, partnerships, or anything else around PK Spot are welcome here.
Have a Google My Maps, KML/KMZ, spreadsheet, or local spot list? Send it over and we can help bring those spots into PK Spot.

notes.note: Button to save the cropped image

segment.source: The options below already match the selected quick filter, so you can fine-tune it instead of starting from scratch.
This is the date you started practicing Parkour and Freerunning. It will be rounded to full years after one year or more so don't sweat it.

⚠️ Important: When embedding this content on your website, you are responsible for obtaining user consent for third-party services (Google Maps, analytics, etc.). Ensure your site has a clear consent banner or terms that users agree to before viewing embedded content.
Sign in to view saved spots
Your bio is public. Avoid private contact details and exact places you train.
Admin tool for adding a new parkour event to PK Spot.
Event creation is currently limited to PK Spot admins. If you'd like to add an event to the map, get in touch with us.
Couldn't create the event. Check the console for details.
Optional. Drop an area on the map to define where the event happens.
Communities that should surface this event. Auto-suggested from the event's area — adjust as needed.
Add parking, info stands, toilets, meetup points, or other temporary event markers.
Delete this event? This can't be undone.
Use a public http or https link.
Use a public http or https link.
Optional. 14–18 is typical for a venue-level view.
Add legacy mobile app area compatibility
Required. Used for the map event pin.
Add paths when this event requires qualification.
Plain ids for filtering/search. Membership ids are added on save.
Add memberships when this event belongs to a tour, qualifier path, or championship.
Event series, qualifier targets, and required paths
Couldn't save the event. Check the console for details.
Add a spot to this event
Add at least three points when this temporary spot has its own area.
Comma-separated. First image becomes the preview.
Use these for temporary builds, stages, training areas, or named zones that should appear only on the event map.
Event-only local spots saved inside this event
Add a main plan to publish a day-by-day event schedule.
Plans, schedule items, linked spots, and linked events
Add schedule blocks, workshops, competitions, travel windows, or alternate-plan items.
Centered on the event pin. Leave empty for no radius promotion.
Promotion is admin-only for now. The radius controls where the event can surface in promoted map-island placements.
This is an interactive map of the event area. Click on blue spots or other pins to see more information.
Qualification requires all of these events
Qualify through one of these events
Qualification is required for this competition.
Couldn't clear your response. Try again in a moment.
These numbers show PK Spot user intent, not tickets bought.
Couldn't save your response. Try again in a moment.
No events match the selected filters.

notes.note: A very short label for the navbar events page button

segment.source: Reset your PK Spot password to get back into your parkour spots account.
Enter your E-mail address and you will receive an E-mail with instructions to reset your password.
This E-Mail address is not valid!
Delete this media item from its spot or event? This action is permanent and will be recorded in moderation provenance.
The reset E-mail has been sent!
Check your inbox and follow the instructions to reset your password.
Error sending reset email. Please try again.
Events in this area will show here.

notes.note: Message shown when user clicks geolocation button but permissions are denied
Snackbar message for disabled geolocation info

segment.source: Location permission denied. Please enable it to use this feature.

notes.note: Action button on snackbar shown when user clicks geolocation button but permissions are denied
Snackbar action for disabled geolocation info
Label of the button to open the website of the spot
Label of the button to delete the spot

segment.source: This spot's data is made available under the CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 license.
Everything on this page is public. Do not add your address, school, private training spots, phone number, or anything that shows where you usually are.
There are no spots matching the filter in this area.
Communities in this area will show here.
PK Spot is the spot for everything parkour. Find spots, check in, discover what is happening nearby, and connect with your local community.
The Spot for Everything Parkour | PK Spot
Find parkour spots near you, check in, discover events and jams, and connect with your local community.
PK Spot is the spot for everything Parkour. Use it to discover spots near you, check in, find events and jams, and connect with your local community.

Every spot can be edited by people who actually train there. You'll find photos, ratings, and tags telling you whether a spot is good for Parkour, Freerunning, dry training, indoor sessions, and more!
PK Spot — find spots, communities, and events near you

notes.note: Message shown before a media file is uploaded to get consent

segment.source: By uploading, you agree to our Terms of Service. You confirm you own this content or have the right to share it. You grant PK Spot the license it needs to operate the service and agree to make your contribution available to the community under a Creative Commons license.
Record this message in provenance and remove it from the active inbox.
Remove this contact message from the active inbox after recording provenance.
Close this report as no further investigation. The report is recorded in provenance and removed from the queue.
Delete this media item from its target and record the action.
Delete the spot with its nested data and record the action.
Record the report as handled, remove it from the queue, and keep the warning on the target.

notes.note: A very short label for the admin moderation page button

segment.source: Please select a file to upload
The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved.
edits are waiting for community voting.
There is already a spot at this location.
No Google account found on this device. Please add a Google account in your device settings and try again.
Could not sign in with Google!
No Apple ID found on this device. Please sign in to your Apple ID in your device settings and try again.
Could not sign in with Apple!
Save favorites and come back to the spots you want to session later.
You have not saved any spots yet.
Failed to update spot organization settings
Delete this contact message from the active inbox? The message will still be recorded in moderation provenance.
You can edit your public profile here. The information on your public profile can be seen by everyone, even unauthenticated visitors.
Learn more about PK Spot, our values, and the people behind the project.
All community spot data on PK Spot is licensed under
This means the data belongs to the community and remains open and shareable.
Manage your account settings including email, password, and account deletion.
No edits yet for this spot.
Permanently delete your account and all associated data. This action cannot be undone.
Are you sure? You will need to sign in with again to confirm deletion.
Are you sure? Enter your password to confirm deletion.
Review spot and media reports from the community.
General settings to tune your experience on PK Spot.
Recent messages from the public contact form.
Manage your profile and account settings.
Built and maintained by volunteers from the Parkour community.
Email changed successfully. Please verify your new email.
Error changing email. Please check your password.
This email is already in use.
Password must be at least 6 characters
Error changing password. Please try again.
Your account has been deleted. Goodbye!
Error deleting account. Please try again.
Security check failed. Please sign in again and try deleting your account.
Find spots, join local communities, discover events and jams, and check in where parkour is happening around you.
Landing in a new city? Find places to train, see what locals rate highly, follow upcoming events, and use community pages to understand the scene around you.
Account mismatch. Please sign in to the correct account.
We believe the map belongs to the athletes, not a corporation. PK Spot is open source, privacy-first, and community owned.
Sign in or create an account
PK Spot works without forcing sign-in first, but an account gives you a better way to contribute and keep track of the spots you care about.
This E-Mail address is not valid!
Sign in to PK Spot or create a free account to find spots, check in, and connect with your local parkour community.
Curious about our values, partners, or team?
Create a free PK Spot account to find spots, check in, and connect with your local parkour community.
Delete this spot and all of its reviews and edits? This cannot be undone.
An unknown error has occured on sign in. Please try again.
The e-mail address needs to be verified later.
The repeated password needs to match the password entered.
What should we call you? Don't worry, the display name can be changed later.

notes.note: Text of the Terms of Service sign up text
terms of service sign up text

segment.source: By creating an account, you agree to our Terms of Service and confirm you meet the age requirements or have parent/guardian consent, and that you will contribute content under the license terms described within.
Sign in to view visited spots
Password and repeated password don't match
You need to agree to the terms and conditions!
One organization controls the official info for a managed venue.
— No black-box algorithms, no hidden tracking. View on GitHub.

notes.note: The tootltip of the share spot icon button

segment.source: Icon button with a directions icon

notes.note: Message shown when a spot has been reported

segment.source: This spot is not eligible for highlights. To be eligible, it needs at least one user-submitted picture and at least one rating.

notes.note: Aria label for delete spot button

segment.source: Delete button with a trash icon

notes.note: Header for the spot details section
Header for the Google Maps section
The header for the spot features and amenities
The header for the spot description

segment.source: Info can be outdated or wrong. Always check signs and local rules on site.

notes.note: The address subtitle in the spot details
Tooltip of button to open the spot in maps

segment.source: Open the location of this spot in Maps

notes.note: Button to open the spot in maps
Tooltip for navigate to spot button
Button text for navigate to spot
Header for the ratings and reviews section
Text showing the number of reviews
Message shown when there are no reviews yet

segment.source: This spot has no reviews yet, be the first to leave one!

notes.note: Tooltip of button to review the spot
Button to review the spot label
Aria label for edit spot button

segment.source: Edit button with a pencil icon
We are open to help with testing, software, spot reviews, translations, design, and local community work.

notes.note: Label of the button to edit the spot

segment.source: Upload photos and videos so people can quickly understand a spot before they go.
View edit history of this spot

notes.note: Aria label for edit history button

segment.source: Edit history button with a history icon

notes.note: Label of the button to view the edit history of the spot
Aria label for report spot button

segment.source: Report button with a warning icon

notes.note: Label of the button to report the spot
Label of the button to discard changes
Label of the button to save changes
Header for spot name editing section
Placeholder of the spot name in the edit form

segment.source: Delete this spot and all of its nested data? This action is permanent and will be recorded in moderation provenance.

notes.note: Message shown when there are media items to reorder

segment.source: You can re-order the spot media below using drag-and-drop.
Save the spot first to add images.
If you have a Google My Maps, KML/KMZ, spreadsheet, or local spot list, we are happy to help import it.

notes.note: The header for the spot description
Placeholder of the spot description edit field

segment.source: Description of the spot, its features and more...
Search Google Places near this spot
Tell us what is wrong with
Add an editable boundary for this spot. After adding, you can adjust the polygon shape on the map.

notes.note: Label of the button to add bounds to a spot
Header for type and access editing section
Label for the spot type selection field

segment.source: Get help with PK Spot — answers to common questions about parkour spots, accounts, and the community.

notes.note: Label for the spot access selection field
Header for spot URL editing section
Label for selecting preferred spot URL

segment.source: lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only
Only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens are allowed.
Follow what others discover, contribute updates, and help keep the map alive.
Newest changes first. Each entry shows exactly what was edited.
Cannot share a spot that hasn't been saved yet
Link to spot copied to clipboard
Check-ins and organization updates show where parkour is actually happening. The map stays current because the community keeps it current.

notes.note: The text in the spot list panel if no spots are found/displayed

segment.source: There are no highlighted spots for this area, zoom in on dots in the map to discover other spots.
What would you like to report this spot for?
It's a private spot and should not be on this spot map.
No organization verifies this public spot yet.
Also block this user after reporting
Which spot is this spot a duplicate of?
Note: Spot reports are public and will be displayed on the spot itself together with your profile and user name.
For private questions, partnerships, spot map imports, or joining the crew, send us a message directly.
What would you rate this spot from one to five stars?
Find answers to common questions or reach out to us directly.
Have a question, found a bug, or want to request a feature? We'd love to hear from you!
Join our Discord server to chat with the community, report bugs, and request features. This is the fastest way to get help!
PK Spot is a free, privacy-first, community-owned map for parkour. The project is built entirely by volunteers to help athletes find spots, connect, and share their local scene.
PK Spot is a free tool built by athletes. To keep the servers running, we partner with the Parkour ecosystem — national associations, ethical brands, and Parkour gyms.
If this list is empty, organization reads are still blocked by rules or no organizations exist yet.
Feel free to slide into our DMs! We're happy to help with quick questions.
Add new spots, fix details, and help keep local knowledge useful for everyone.
Can't find what you're looking for? Join our Discord and we'll help you out!
PK Spot helps you find parkour spots, discover communities and events, check in, and share useful local knowledge.
Respect property, local laws, people, and the places you train. Leave no trace.
Parkour is risky. You are responsible for your safety. Spot data may be wrong, unsafe, or not legally accessible.
Only share places and content when you have all needed rights and permissions. By contributing, you license them to PK Spot so we can run the service.
Some events may be marked as Promoted because PK Spot has a paid or otherwise material relationship with the organizer. Other events appear because they are relevant to your area or timing.
After you agree, PK Spot loads essential services like Google Maps, which may use cookies. Analytics stay cookie-free. This site is protected by reCAPTCHA.

By choosing "Agree and Continue", you accept our Terms of Service and acknowledge our Privacy Policy. You confirm you are at least 13 and old enough to consent under your local law, or that a parent or guardian agrees for you.
Please select the reason for this report:

source.pc: Not relevant: This media does not belong here or is misleading.
Bad quality: This media is blurry, too dark, or generally poor quality.
Duplicate: This media is already present.
Copyright infringement: This is my content and I did not give permission for it to be used here.
I'm visible and have a problem with it.
This media violates the terms of service in other ways (nudity, violence, etc.)

segment.source: We'll only use your email to contact you about this report.
Your display name is public. Use a nickname if you prefer.
Parkour Spots, Events & Communities | PK Spot
Optional and public. Leave it blank if you do not want to share it.
You have not marked any spots as visited yet.
Report submitted. Thanks for helping keep spots accurate.
Is this an indoor or outdoor spot?

notes.note: A very short label for the navbar get app button

segment.source: Is there a water feature (fountain, river, lake etc.)?
Is there drinking water available right at the spot?
This is a sketchy or mental challenge that requires physical and emotional control
The goal of this challenge is to be creative. This challenge may also be more open to interpretation.
This techy challenge requires great precision and control.
A core challenge of this spot. An essential challenge for anyone seeking to master this spot.
This spot is a dedicated indoor Parkour training facility with focused equipment and obstacles. Venue rules, hours, and required permission should be checked before training.
This spot is a commercial trampoline-focused venue with aerial training areas. On-site safety rules, booking terms, and staff instructions apply.
This spot is a gymnastics-oriented facility where access and use may depend on supervision, classes, or permission.
This spot is a purpose-built outdoor Parkour area designed for training. Posted rules and shared community use apply.
This spot is a playground space that can support movement training. Child safety, shared use, and local guidance are especially important.
This spot is on school grounds or at an education facility where access can change by schedule and institutional rules.
This spot is in a general park environment with mixed public use where local park regulations apply.
This spot is in a built city environment where pedestrian flow, nearby property boundaries, and local restrictions must be considered.
This spot is in a nature-based area where environmental impact should be minimized and seasonal or conservation restrictions may apply.
This spot is on university or college grounds where some areas may be open while others require permission.
This spot is an art or cultural structure where training may be possible. Respect, preservation, and site-specific rules are essential.
This spot is in an elevated roof area with high access, safety, and legal risk where permission should be verified first.
This spot involves movement between separate elevated structures where access, safety, and legality must be clearly confirmed before training.
This spot is mainly defined by descending movement where landings, exits, and public impact should be assessed beforehand.
This spot is at a monument or heritage location where respectful behavior and cultural, legal, and preservation rules are essential.
This spot is centered around water where conditions can change quickly and safety plus local restrictions must be verified on arrival.
This spot is in a parking structure where vehicles, security policies, and time-based access limits need to be considered.
This spot is a skate-focused shared facility where local etiquette and posted rules should be followed and right of way respected.
This spot is a fitness-equipment area with shared-use expectations where equipment should be used respectfully and according to guidance.
This spot does not fit listed categories. Specifics can be captured through amenities and the main spot description.

notes.note: No media placeholder label shown on spot preview cards

segment.source: People usually can access this spot. Check signs, hours, and local rules when you arrive.
You usually need to pay, book, or have a membership to use this spot. Follow staff and venue rules.
This spot is in a residential area. Access may be limited if you are not a resident or guest.
This spot is on private property. Ask for permission before training here.
This spot should not be used unless you have clear permission from the owner or manager.
Access is unclear for this spot. Check locally before training.
